steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Left of blank: independent clause -- "The human shoulder contains the infraspinatus, a skeletal muscle that is attached to the scapula." Right of blank: independent clause -- "this muscle plays a role in rotating the upper arm." Two independent clauses must be joined with a semicolon, period, or coordinating conjunction; a comma alone creates a comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A -- scapula,: "...attached to the scapula, this muscle plays a role..." Comma between two independent clauses = comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B -- scapula;: "...attached to the scapula; this muscle plays a role in rotating the upper arm." Semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice C -- scapula: "...attached to the scapula this muscle plays a role..." No punctuation between two independent clauses = run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice D -- scapula that: "...attached to the scapula that this muscle plays a role in rotating the upper arm." "That" turns the second clause into a relative clause modifying "scapula," which is illogical -- a scapula is not something that a muscle plays a role in rotating. ✗
That matches choice B -- B is correct.
